title: 2024 Marketplace plan with premium tax credit
meta-title: 2024 Marketplace plan with premium tax credit
meta-description: Learn how your healthcare insurance will impact your 2024 federal tax filing. What you need to know for maximum return, premium tax credit, and more.
page_name: 2024 Marketplace plan with premium tax credit
excerpt: Learn how your healthcare insurance will impact your 2024 federal tax filing. What you need to know for maximum return, premium tax credit, and more.
content:
You must file a federal tax return for 2024 — even if you usually don't file or your income is below the level requiring you to.

1. Find your Form 1095-A, Health Insurance Marketplace® Statement

Log into your account — select your 2024 application, then select "Tax forms."

Get help finding your 1095-A.

Don’t file your 2024 taxes until you have an accurate 1095-A.

2. Make sure your 1095-A is correct — and if not, call us

Confirm basic information about health plan and household members on Parts I and II of the form. Contact the Marketplace Call Center if you find an error.

Make sure the premium for your second lowest cost Silver plan (SLCSP) is correct. If it's not, use our tax tool to get your SLCSP.

Learn how to check your SLCSP.

3. Fill out Form 8962 to reconcile

  • Use the information from your 1095-A to fill out Form 8962, Premium Tax Credit.
  • Use Form 8962 to reconcile your premium tax credit — compare the amount you used in 2024 to lower your monthly plan premium with the actual premium tax credit you qualify for based on your final 2024 income. Any difference will affect your refund or tax owed.

Get a step-by-step guide to reconciling your premium tax credit.

4. Attach Form 8962 when you file your federal income tax return

Don't attach your 1095-A to your return. Keep both your 1095-A and a copy of Form 8962 with your other tax documents.

Tax forms and guides

  • Guide to using Form 1095-A
  • IRS information on Form 1095-A
  • Step-by-step guide to reconciling your premium tax credit
  • Health coverage tax tool (use only if your second lowest cost Silver plan (SLCSP) is missing or wrong)
  • Form 1040, U.S. Individual Income Tax Return (PDF, 159 KB) and Form 1040 instructions (PDF, 4.2 MB)
  • Form 8962, Premium Tax Credit (PDF, 115 KB) and Form 8962 instructions (PDF, 387 KB)

More than one type of coverage?

If you had a Marketplace plan with the premium tax credit for only part of 2024, or other household members had different coverage, select the other coverage:

  • Marketplace plan without the premium tax credit
  • Job-based health coverage
  • Other health coverage
  • No health coverage
